body_txt: There are some downsides to being wealthy. Rich people can't go out and enjoy a regular, laid-back evening on the town, especially if they are a celebrity figure . Regardless if they are a Rich Bitch or Spoiled Sweet , both are scorned by the average man. People are either resentful or revealed to be a Pretty Freeloader or Gold Digger . In addition to other reasons some savvy rich, elite characters will hide their wealth. They may have a net worth that ranks in the Fiction 500 but have an Affluent Ascetic lifestyle in a simple suburban two-bedroom home living a life Beneath Notice . This creates an added surprise when their Arbitrarily Large Bank Account comes into play and may justify a One-Hour Work Week . Expect these characters to be kind, caring and generally the opposite of a Rich Bitch , so that the reveal of their wealth is more shocking. For some, they've even been separated from the family and merely have name recognition without actual money, and with little desire to regain it. Alternatively, a common joke will be that other characters question why they never pick up the tab at group dinners. In Real Life , there are several advantages to attempting this. Large wealth disparity tends to create serious rifts in casual social interaction; friends of a lower-income class may secretly resent you, or expect large monetary gifts and favors. Modest living and heavy charitable donations are also a method of avoiding excessive taxes. On the other side of the coin, it is also hard to know who likes you for yourself, and who only likes you for your money (this is a reason many rich people only cavort amongst their own class). In addition, those who are known to be wealthy are frequent targets for lawsuits , and family members may even be targeted for kidnapping by those looking for a ransom. Hiding your wealth can prevent many problems like these. With all that being said, this trope is No Real Life Examples, Please! . Compare King Incognito (where the secret might be some other type of power besides wealth), Slumming It (where this is done for fun ), and Non-Idle Rich (for when the wealthy person works a "regular" job for fulfillment and/or helping others). Contrast Mock Millionaire , which is a character who is not as wealthy as they claim to be in an attempt to build prestige.
